name: Pino Monumental – running circular from Moscardón through Prado Molinero
name_original: Pino Monumental – running circular desde Moscardón por el Prado Molinero
region_intro: Jogging routes around Moscardón are set within Spain's Sierra de Albarracín, offering diverse terrain for runners. The region features natural scenery, including pine forests, river systems, and varied landscapes. Elevation changes are present, with some routes featuring moderate climbs, while others remain largely flat. This environment provides a natural backdrop for physical activity. Best jogging routes around Moscardón The most popular jogging route is Ojos del Cabriel and cascada del Molino de San Pedro – circular by La Herrería Waterfall, a 4.6 miles (7.5 km) trail that takes 55 minutes to complete. This route explores natural water features and offers scenic views. Another top favourite among local runners is Mirador del Alto de las Peñas – circular from Terriente, a moderate 6.7 miles (10.7 km) path. This route provides panoramic views from elevated points. Local runners also love the Pino Monumental – running circular from Moscardón through Prado Molinero, a 5.5 miles (8.9 km) trail leading through woodlands, often completed in about 1 hour 7 minutes. Jogging around Moscardón is defined by pine forests, river valleys, and gentle hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging trails with elevation gain. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 60 runners have used komoot to explore Moscardón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Moscardón?
A: There are at least 9 dedicated running routes around Moscardón listed on komoot, offering a variety of experiences for joggers. These routes are set within the picturesque Sierra de Albarracín, providing diverse terrain and natural scenery.

Q: Are there running routes in Moscardón suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, the Sierra de Albarracín, where Moscardón is located, features 'Rutas Trail-Running Verdes' which are easy trails, typically under 6 kilometers with less than 200 meters of elevation gain. These are ideal for beginners and families looking for a gentle introduction to mountain running in a natural setting. While specific easy routes are not detailed in the guide, the region generally offers varied terrain suitable for different fitness levels.

Q: What kind of natural features or landmarks can I expect to see while jogging in Moscardón?
A: Moscardón's running routes showcase the stunning natural beauty of the Sierra de Albarracín. You can expect to encounter pine forests, river systems, and captivating waterfalls. Notable natural attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247809" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Herrería Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/604533" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Pedro Mill Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4547251" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos del Cabriel&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features unique red sandstone landscapes, such as those found in the Pinares de Rodeno.

Q: Are there any circular running routes around Moscardón?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983328144/pino-monumental-running-circular-from-moscardon-through-prado-molinero" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pino Monumental – running circular from Moscardón through Prado Molinero&lt;/a&gt;, which is 8.9 km long and takes you through beautiful woodlands.

Q: Can I find routes with scenic viewpoints for jogging in Moscardón?
A: Absolutely. The region offers routes with panoramic views. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983323510/mirador-del-alto-de-las-penas-circular-from-terriente" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Alto de las Peñas – circular from Terriente&lt;/a&gt; is a 10.7 km route specifically designed to provide stunning vistas from elevated points. The Ermita de San Roque, visible along some trails, also offers beautiful views of the locality.

Q: What is the terrain like on the running trails in Moscardón?
A: The terrain around Moscardón is varied, offering a dynamic experience for runners. You'll find a mix of pine forests, river valleys, and gentle hills. Some routes are largely flat, while others feature moderate climbs with elevation gains, providing options for different preferences and fitness levels. This diverse landscape helps improve balance, agility, and strength.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by water features like rivers or waterfalls?
A: Yes, several routes in the Moscardón area incorporate natural water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983156660/ojos-del-cabriel-and-cascada-del-molino-de-san-pedro-circular-by-la-herreria-waterfall" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos del Cabriel and cascada del Molino de San Pedro – circular by La Herrería Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, exploring natural springs and waterfalls. The region is also home to the Barranco de la Hoz (White River) and the Nacimiento del Río Tajo (source of the Tagus River), which add to the scenic beauty.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Moscardón?
A: Runners frequently praise the stunning natural scenery and the variety of terrain available. The low-population density natural environment allows for a serene and invigorating experience, connecting with nature. The mix of woodlands, river valleys, and viewpoints provides an engaging and less monotonous outdoor cardio workout.

Q: Are there any longer running routes for more experienced runners?
A: For those seeking a longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983315048/fuente-del-buey-local-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Buey Local Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 13 km route. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983323510/mirador-del-alto-de-las-penas-circular-from-terriente" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Alto de las Peñas – circular from Terriente&lt;/a&gt; offers a substantial 10.7 km run with panoramic views.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Moscardón?
A: Moscardón offers a beautiful setting for running throughout much of the year. The diverse terrain and natural environment are enjoyable in various seasons. While specific seasonal recommendations aren't provided, the region's natural beauty makes outdoor sport appealing, especially when temperatures are mild. Always check local weather conditions before heading out.

Q: Are there any challenging running routes with significant elevation gain?
A: Yes, for runners looking for a more demanding experience, Moscardón and the surrounding Sierra de Albarracín offer routes with notable elevation changes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31447470" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva del diablo loop from Calomarde&lt;/a&gt; is classified as difficult, providing a good test of endurance with its climbs and descents.
tips: Wonderful place in a remote area within the Universal Mountains. A few meters away is the Fuente de la Muela and a few km from the town of Moscardón. Here the tranquility and nature reign, the sound of the birds and the silence as well as the pure air.

Monumental specimen of black pine (Pinus nigra) with a height of 30 m and a trunk perimeter of 3.60 meters. With a straight trunk, the branches develop in the crown and at mid-height. It is located in the area of La Majada, isolated, and a few meters away is a forest refuge.
